goal: Sync's scanMainNotes walks the notes ref directly (git notes list) instead of the last N commits on main. Notes on commits past Check.Lookback no longer silently strand intents in proposed. Reachability filter via merge-base --is-ancestor keeps stray notes on non-main commits from polluting the view.

summary.title: Sync's note scan walks the notes ref directly — no more Lookback drop-off
summary.what: Replaces scanMainNotes' LogOneline(mainBranch, Check.Lookback) loop with a Git.NotesListCommits() loop. Per-commit merge-base --is-ancestor filter keeps notes on non-main commits from being treated as merged. Commits are sorted by CommitDate before processing so the chronological replay invariant (later revert overrides earlier merge for the same intent) is preserved. Adds two integration tests: one that sets Lookback=2 + 5 filler commits + asserts the seed intent's note is still picked up (the dogfood failure mode); one that hand-adds a mainline note to a feature-branch tip + asserts sync does NOT mark that intent as merged.
summary.why: Dogfood failure: int_5c0800d7 (the original mvp intent, sealed on day 1) silently regressed from merged back to proposed once main accumulated more than 50 commits. The note on its merge commit (d892344) was intact and pushed; the bug was that scanMainNotes only walked the most recent Lookback commits on main, so older notes fell off the window. Any active project will hit this within a few weeks; for the friend about to use Mainline this would have looked like a random data-loss bug. Walking the notes ref directly makes Lookback irrelevant to merged-status determination — the notes ref IS the source of truth, sync's job is to honour every entry on it.
summary.user_goal: Make sync's view of merged status correct regardless of how deep main has grown.

decisions.chose: git notes --ref=mainline/intents list driving the loop, with merge-base --is-ancestor filter per commit
decisions.rationale: Notes ref is the canonical source. Walking it directly means correctness is independent of main-branch depth. The reachability filter prevents stray notes (manual pin to wrong commit, feature branch tip with a hand-attached note) from being trusted.
rejected: Bump Lookback to 1000 — buys time but the bug recurs at 1001 commits.
Cache merged status persistently in the view — risks staleness on revert and adds a second source of truth.

decisions.rationale: Cheap (~10ms each), correct, simple. For repos with many notes (>1000) we may want to switch to a single git rev-list traversal but that's a future micro-optimisation; current notes counts are dozens, not thousands.
Already used elsewhere (intentViewActivityAt). Cheaper than parsing notes for their added_at timestamps and more correct because the commit's own date is what determines the ordering on main.
rejected: note.AddedAt — the note timestamp is when the pin was written, not when the code landed; can be much later than the commit date.

risks: Per-commit merge-base call adds N git invocations on every sync. With 100 notes that's ~1s. Acceptable now; if it becomes the dominant cost we can batch via a single git rev-list intersection.
If a user manually deletes the notes ref and expects sync to honour git log order instead, this change breaks them — but no such user exists; the notes ref has been canonical since rc3.

If the notes ref contains entries for commits in a parallel-history repo (rare; happens with submodule extraction or filter-branch), the reachability filter might falsely accept or reject. Documented in the function comment but not load-bearing.
followups: If per-commit merge-base ever shows up as a sync bottleneck, batch via `git rev-list <main> -- | grep -F` style intersection.
The lookback config field is now unused by scanMainNotes — keep it for the auto-pin reconcile cascade (still uses LogOneline) but document the per-call usage.

architectural_claims: git notes ref is the authoritative source for merged status; sync iterates every entry on it.
Reachability of a noted commit from main is verified per commit via merge-base.
behavioral_changes: Notes attached to commits older than Check.Lookback are now honoured by sync.
Notes attached to commits not in main's ancestry are silently ignored (previously they couldn't be reached anyway).
